mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate above 5% keeps discount rates high, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing below-average S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains restrictive.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-04-01 vintage, highest since 2001
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during periods with FEDFUNDS > 5% are not significantly lower than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points or more over a 3-month window (a Sahm-rule-like threshold) signals an imminent recessionary labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as markets price in earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 3-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% as of 2024-04-01, near multi-decade lows
failure_condition: In historical instances where UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp in 3 months, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in industrial production growth and retail sales growth signals a broad-based demand-supply slowdown that is not visible from either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both weaken together.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0 AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised down from 102.66 to 100.46 vintage in March 2024
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS growth conditions are met jointly, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either series alone (delta Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate regime (FEDFUNDS above 5%), declining housing starts reflect acute rate sensitivity in the most cyclical demand sector, foreshadowing broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is muted or absent when rates are low.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised to 1312K units March 2024 vintage, FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy rate
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS > 5%, declining HOUST fails to precede negative 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the effect reverses sign relative to low-rate regimes with statistical significance p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production reflects healthy real-economy output growth that supports near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive INDPRO momentum are not significantly higher than following negative momentum (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 0.5% signals abrupt consumer spending weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as consumption comprises the majority of GDP and equity earnings exposure.
failure_condition: Following RSAFS monthly declines exceeding 0.5%, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds trailing headline CPI inflation growth (a positive real policy rate), the combination of tight monetary policy and moderating inflation raises real borrowing costs enough to compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this effect requires both conditions jointly and does not hold when real rates are negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL year-over-year percent change > 1.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 5.33% vs CPIAUCSL 313.02 revised April 2024 vintage implying elevated real rate
failure_condition: When the real rate exceeds 1.5pp, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint interaction signal shows no incremental Rank IC over FEDFUNDS alone (delta < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with unemployment near historic lows, continued strong nonfarm payroll growth signals labor market overheating risk that raises the odds of hawkish monetary tightening surprises, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; in early-cycle recoveries the same payroll growth signal is positive for returns because it confirms recovery strength rather than overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE 3.9% and PAYEMS 158286K thousand persons as of April 2024 vintage
failure_condition: During late-cycle low-unemployment periods, strong PAYEMS growth precedes positive 5-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the direction fails to reverse relative to early-cycle recovery periods with statistical significance p < 0.05.
